steps.title: Financial Intelligence with AI
steps.body:
Money management is data management — and AI loves data.

Today you'll learn how AI transforms personal and business finance: automated budgeting, expense categorization, financial forecasting, and investment research.

You don't need a finance degree. You need good prompts and the right data.

Financial intelligence isn't about knowing every number — it's about knowing which numbers matter.

AI can process thousands of transactions, identify patterns, and surface insights that would take hours to find manually. The operator's job: ask the right questions about the numbers that matter.

steps.title: Scenario: The Freelancer's Financial Fog
steps.body:
Maria is a freelance designer. She earns well but has no idea where her money goes.

Each month: $6k-$12k in revenue (varies wildly). She has 3 recurring subscription services for design tools, a co-working membership, client lunch meetings, and software purchases. Plus quarterly tax payments she keeps forgetting.

She has 6 months of bank statements in CSV format. What should she do with AI?

steps.question: What's Maria's best first step with AI?
options: Ask AI to predict next year's income with no data
Upload her 6 months of CSV statements and ask AI to categorize expenses and identify spending patterns
Manually enter every transaction into a spreadsheet first, then ask AI to review
Ask AI to create a budget without looking at past spending
feedback: AI needs data to predict. Without historical data, it's guessing.
Exactly. Raw data → AI analysis → actionable insights. Let AI do the heavy lifting of categorization.
That's busywork. AI can process raw CSVs. Why do the manual step?
A budget without historical context is just a wish. Let AI analyze the past first.

steps.title: AI Budget Analysis Prompt
steps.body:
Here's how to turn AI into your financial analyst:

"I'm attaching my last 3 months of bank transactions as a CSV. Categories included: [categories]. My monthly income ranges from [X] to [Y].

Please:
1. Categorize all uncategorized transactions
2. Identify my top 5 spending categories by percentage
3. Flag any unusual or suspicious charges
4. Calculate my average monthly burn rate
5. Identify opportunities to save at least 10%"

steps.question: AI can accurately forecast your monthly budget after analyzing 2-3 months of transaction data.
feedback: Correct. With consistent categorization and enough data points, AI can identify patterns and produce reliable forecasts — though it gets more accurate with more data.
Actually, AI can do this. Three months of categorized transactions gives AI enough signal to identify patterns and predict future spending within a useful margin.

steps.question: In AI-powered finance, the operator's value is in asking the right _____ about the numbers.
feedback: Perfect. AI processes numbers, but you ask the questions that turn data into decisions.
Close — think about what drives the conversation between you and the AI.

steps.question: When using AI for investment research, which approach is most effective?
options: Ask AI 'What stocks should I buy?' — let it pick winners
Give AI company metrics and ask it to compare fundamentals, risks, and market position
Only look at AI's stock predictions from 6 months ago
Don't use AI for investing — it's all hype
feedback: AI isn't a stock picker. Asking for specific tickers without context is a misuse of the tool.
Right. AI excels at comparative analysis. Give it fundamentals, ask for structured comparison, and make your own decisions.
AI predictions aren't guarantees. Use AI for research, not fortune-telling.
AI won't pick stocks for you, but it's excellent for research and analysis. Don't dismiss a powerful tool.

steps.title: Financial Pattern Analysis
steps.body:
Imagine you have a monthly income of $7,500 and these expenses: Rent $2,000, Food $600, Subscriptions $120, Transport $300, Entertainment $400, Savings $500, Shopping $350, Utilities $200, Insurance $250, Misc $180.

Ask AI to analyze this budget for optimization opportunities. Ask it to recommend a revised budget that saves an additional 10% without being painful.
steps.prompt: Monthly income: $7,500. Expenses: Rent $2,000, Food $600, Subscriptions $120, Transport $300, Entertainment $400, Savings $500, Shopping $350, Utilities $200, Insurance $250, Misc $180. Analyze this budget. Identify potential savings of 10% without drastic cuts. Suggest a revised budget allocation. Flag any red flags or missing categories.

steps.title: Build Your Financial Prompt Pack
steps.body:
Create a set of 3 financial prompts you'd use regularly:
1. Monthly budget review
2. Expense anomaly detection
3. Savings goal tracker

Test each with sample data in your AI. Refine until you get actionable outputs every time.
steps.deliverable: Three financial analysis prompts that consistently produce actionable insights.
steps.template:
Create a set of 3 financial prompts you'd use regularly:
1. Monthly budget review
2. Expense anomaly detection
3. Savings goal tracker

Test each with sample data in your AI. Refine until you get actionable outputs every time.

steps.title: Paste Your Best Financial Prompt
steps.body: Copy your favorite financial prompt here — the one that gives you the most actionable insights.
steps.prompt: Paste the financial prompt that produces your best results. This is your go-to financial analysis tool.

steps.title: Your Financial AI Future
steps.body:
Think about your current financial habits. What's one area where AI analysis could make a meaningful difference in your financial health? What information would you need to start?

What's holding you back from using AI for personal finance today?

questions.question: What's the most important principle for AI-powered financial intelligence?
options: The more expensive the AI model, the better the financial advice
AI works best when fed your real data and asked specific, structured questions
AI should replace your accountant entirely
Financial AI only works for businesses, not individuals

steps.title: Day 23 Complete: Financial Intelligence
steps.body:
You've turned AI into your personal financial analyst — from budget categorization to investment research to forecasting.

Key insight: Financial AI isn't about asking for stock tips. It's about feeding your real data and asking structured questions that produce actionable answers.

Tomorrow: Creativity & Innovation with AI. Time to make AI your creative partner.
